title: ranunculus sceleratus var. umbellatus
intro: Ranunculus sceleratus var. umbellatus, also known as the sharp-leaved buttercup or water crowfoot, is a plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is characterized by its distinctive umbrella-like clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus sceleratus var. umbellatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is also added to poultices and compresses to treat various health conditions.
